textByHashes.d46b2eb8723fda1795fcd237f904fd49a7887492: Hi there. So much to see here!
textByHashes.7198932f886546493b0c42a576ae91a2d5290edd: Yes, I just learned more about some priestly warriors and a staff of the gods.
textByHashes.78fbf89d2d01e0c5d00b908ea658a30186ea1015: Ahh, I'm big on quests me, love questing. So when I've done one, I check here with 'Historian' Minas to see if I can help the Museum out with some information. Sometimes they already know it, or don't want to know,
textByHashes.ea426cafc07cf55736011b067fc867424db33410: and sometimes it earns me a reward or two.
textByHashes.240334cbf2c8bb6e1afe3ddad28270400a2eee68: You should, as well as everything else there is here in the Museum. Did you know they have a really fun- looking display down in the basement? I love monkeys.
textByHashes.ac9206726e1eba534ee11eca5d1fa86c9b54441c: Oh, just taking a look at these displays. I'm a bit of a history buff.
textByHashes.c18b528d31d1d17a42928ba7810cba9843007508: Well, did you know that Bob the Cat is... no, no... you'd never believe me anyway.
textByHashes.1ba1db34a117f5d48608914ebcefbdf11878aca0: No, no. Couldn't possibly ruin the surprise.
textByHashes.38da81397b1e2ae1c4e9bc403e50409e90574657: If you really want to know, go talk to Unferth in his house in Burthorpe.
textByHashes.bd705ee61ff7b1e7654880be3bf471e7aba4ea21: Oh, hello. I was just looking at this display here.
textByHashes.9cf5d9961f64ae87e77700a3aa4e5da0160f40ae: Yes, one I just updated by talking to 'Historian' Minas. I'm a bit of a quester you see.
textByHashes.7173c37285310e3e38d22c8b432a16d24b99a229: Hmm, yes. Okay. Don't forget to check out the Natural History exhibit - you could learn a lot.
textByHashes.f6a7718e1085b9252f1aace69cbd899b0ed392d5: Yes, good aren't they! Have you done the quest?
textByHashes.0ed1501600fff52ce41fc795724052d34d138776: Oh you should, it lets you get into that bit where they clean the finds, and you can join in!
textByHashes.4307e71ba4feb5ba4dc15d26c2bd0a07d7212d78: Did you know after the quest you can get the teddy bear back by stealing it from the female student?
textByHashes.d644e175c10bde0cf8267317e8205671e23d2b8c: Yes, that's it, just like that. Mustn't forget the map either....hmm...what else?
textByHashes.43b60e737364bfdea1e4391ce1e6ba2ad57d3150: Oh, hello there. Sorry, was a bit engrosed in updating this display. I've just been out questing and thought I'd stop off here you see. It's quite rewarding too.
textByHashes.d7cf4c8f66488e04f9898f40a318397c590d73af: No offence intended, my dear chap. Just very interested in this history stuff, that's all.
textByHashes.999e4dce3bbd4eb550308b043bf5d785bfb1170c: Oh, yes, marvellous isn't it! I particularly like the wyvern, very interesting considering they've never found a live specimen. And to think they could possibly have been flapping around at one time!
textByHashes.66ef464ebdc1caff3198fdb901b145edd3fc6f4b: Yes, very intricate isn't it? Worrying to think some people risk their lives in them! You wouldn't catch me up in one. Had a chat with Jacques while I was up there, the art critic...he's a bit funny in the head.
textByHashes.8cdeb450ce8bda7e1d2339599d50a14fc8c325a8: Well, he thinks he's going to get an entire portrait gallery to himself!
textByHashes.e35d04e0a830addffbd990bf5a1cb82822a187c1: I guess so. Wonder what poetry he'll come up with then! Still, I must get on with this display. See you around I hope!
